title: Symplectic Cohomology Construction Via C*-actions
speaker: Filip Zivanovic
abstract: In a series of papers with Alexander Ritter, we construct a symplectic cohomology for open non-convex symplectic manifolds that admit pseudo-holomorphic C*-actions. Out of this construction, we get a filtration on ordinary cohomology of these manifolds, which in practice is explicitly computable, using Morse-Bott-Floer spectral sequences. I will show concrete examples of ADE resolutions and low-dimensional Higgs bundles. If time permits, I will comment on the comparison with other known filtrations for the latter spaces.

proof_stages.label: Classical Floer and Liouville symplectic cohomology
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls Hamiltonian Floer complexes and the standard Liouville/convex-at-infinity construction of symplectic cohomology via linear Hamiltonians, maximum principles, continuation maps, and a direct limit.

proof_stages.label: C^n as the C*-action model
proof_stages.summary: For C^n the scalar C*-moment map is the radial coordinate, clean irrational-slope Hamiltonians have only the origin as a periodic orbit and force SH(C^n)=0 by grading, while convex Hamiltonians yield orbit families and a Morse-Bott spectral sequence whose cancellations motivate the general construction.

proof_stages.label: Symplectic C*-manifolds and the core
proof_stages.summary: The proposed replacement for a radial coordinate is the S^1 moment map of a holomorphic Hamiltonian contracting C*-action, whose fixed locus and core have compactness, deformation-retraction, and cohomological localization properties.

proof_stages.label: Analytic compactness from a proper convex base
proof_stages.summary: Since a contracting C*-action alone does not give a maximum principle or dissipativity, the construction imposes a proper compatible map to a convex base so Floer compactness descends from the base, a condition satisfied by many algebraic examples.

proof_stages.label: Existence, action-independence, and Calabi-Yau vanishing of SH
proof_stages.summary: For symplectic C*-manifolds over a convex base one obtains unital symplectic cohomology and products, action-independence for commuting algebraic actions, and vanishing in the Calabi-Yau case by tracking the grading of fixed-orbit clean Hamiltonians.

proof_stages.label: Quantum-cohomology filtration and Seidel control
proof_stages.summary: Kernels of continuation maps from small Hamiltonians to slope lambda define an action-dependent ideal filtration on quantum cohomology, whose integer levels are described by multiplication by Seidel elements and hence relate SH to a localization of QH.

proof_stages.label: Comparison with Atiyah-Bott fixed-point filtrations
proof_stages.summary: The Floer filtration is compared with the classical Atiyah-Bott filtration by moment-map levels, with the key distinction that Floer theory can split different cohomology classes lying in the same fixed component.

proof_stages.label: Morse-Bott spectral sequence for computing the filtration
proof_stages.summary: Convex C*-Hamiltonians admit a modified action filtration whose E1 page is built from local Floer cohomology of constant, rational-isotropy, and integral orbit families, and a class lies in a given filtration level exactly when it is killed by columns up to that period.

examples.name: Cotangent bundles and loop-space homology
examples.note: The Viterbo isomorphism for a cotangent bundle is used to illustrate that symplectic cohomology of an open symplectic manifold can recover a rich ring, namely loop-space homology with string product.

examples.name: Symplectic resolutions motivating the convex-base hypothesis
examples.note: Projective symplectic resolutions of affine singularities, including examples from geometric representation theory, are presented as the source of the proper-over-a-convex-base condition that makes the Floer analysis work.

examples.name: A2 resolution with several C*-actions

examples.engagement_reason: the A2 resolution is worked through for several C*-actions, including fixed loci, weights, rational-period orbit families, spectral-sequence columns, and resulting filtration pieces

examples.name: Quiver varieties and Higgs-bundle moduli
examples.note: These later applications show that the Floer-theoretic filtration can be compared with representation-theoretic and perverse filtrations in algebraic-geometric moduli problems.
